questions.question: On dividing $x^3 - 3x^2 + x + 2$ by a polynomial $g(x)$, the quotient and remainder were $x - 2$ and $-2x + 4$, respectively. Find $g(x)$.

steps: Use the division algorithm formula: $p(x) = g(x) \cdot q(x) + r(x)$.
Subtract the remainder from the dividend: $(x^3 - 3x^2 + x + 2) - (-2x + 4) = x^3 - 3x^2 + 3x - 2$.
Divide the result by $(x - 2)$ to obtain $g(x)$.

questions.question: Find the values of $a$ and $b$ if the polynomial $x^4 + x^3 + 8x^2 + ax + b$ is exactly divisible by $x^2 + 1$.

steps: Perform long division of $x^4 + x^3 + 8x^2 + ax + b$ by $x^2 + 1$.
Identify the remainder as a linear expression in terms of $a$ and $b$.
Since it is exactly divisible, set the remainder to zero.
solution.derivation: (x^4 + x^3 + 8x^2 + ax + b) = (x^2 + 1)(x^2 + x + 7) + (a-1)x + (b-7). \text{ For remainder to be } 0, a-1=0 \implies a=1 \text{ and } b-7=0 \implies b=7.

questions.question: Divide the polynomial $p(x) = 3x^2 - x^3 - 3x + 5$ by $g(x) = x - 1 - x^2$ and verify the division algorithm.

steps: Arrange the terms in descending order of powers: $p(x) = -x^3 + 3x^2 - 3x + 5$ and $g(x) = -x^2 + x - 1$.
Check the result using $p(x) = g(x) \cdot q(x) + r(x)$.
solution.derivation: \frac{-x^3 + 3x^2 - 3x + 5}{-x^2 + x - 1} = (x - 2) \text{ with remainder } 3. \text{ Verification: } (-x^2 + x - 1)(x - 2) + 3 = -x^3 + 2x^2 + x^2 - 2x - x + 2 + 3 = -x^3 + 3x^2 - 3x + 5.

questions.question: What must be subtracted from $8x^4 + 14x^3 - 2x^2 + 7x - 8$ so that the resulting polynomial is exactly divisible by $4x^2 + x - 2$?

steps: Calculate the remainder of the division.
The remainder is the value that must be subtracted to make the polynomial divisible.
solution.derivation: (8x^4 + 14x^3 - 2x^2 + 7x - 8) \div (4x^2 + x - 2) = 2x^2 + 3x - 2 \text{ quotient and } 2x - 12 \text{ remainder. Thus, subtract } 2x - 12.

questions.question: Obtain all zeros of $3x^4 + 6x^3 - 2x^2 - 10x - 5$, if two of its zeros are $\sqrt{5/3}$ and $-\sqrt{5/3}$.

steps: Multiply by 3 to get $3x^2 - 5$ as a factor.
Divide the original polynomial by $3x^2 - 5$ to get the quotient $x^2 + 2x + 1$.
Factorize the quotient to find the remaining zeros.
